mnemonics.title: FIRST and FOLLOW
mnemonics.content: For FIRST: Find terminals that START any derivation. For FOLLOW: Find terminals that FOLLOW a non-terminal in sentential forms.

mnemonics.title: LL(1) Meaning
mnemonics.content: L - Left to right scan, L - Leftmost derivation, (1) - 1 token lookahead

mnemonics.title: Top-Down vs Bottom-Up
mnemonics.content: TOP = Tree builds from ROOT (start symbol); BOTTOM = Tree builds from LEAVES (input tokens)

mnemonics.title: LL(1) Conflicts
mnemonics.content: LEFT recursion and COMMON prefixes create CONFLICTS in LL(1)

mnemonics.title: Parse Table Action
mnemonics.content: When cell (NonTerminal, Terminal) has entry: PUSH that production's RHS to stack

acronyms.expansion: Left-to-right scan, Leftmost derivation, 1 token lookahead
acronyms.usage: Class of grammars that can be parsed predictively

acronyms.expansion: Set of terminals that appear FIRST in derivations
acronyms.usage: Used to compute predictive parse table entries

acronyms.expansion: Set of terminals that FOLLOW a non-terminal
acronyms.usage: Used for ε-productions and parse table construction
The body of a production rule (what it expands to)
Marker at end of input stream used in FOLLOW sets

visualTips.visualization: Draw a tree showing all derivations from a symbol and collect only the FIRST (leftmost) terminals at each branch tip
Matrix with non-terminals as rows (Y-axis) and terminals as columns (X-axis); find row for non-terminal, column for current input, intersection is production
Think of a cafeteria stack: push items (grammar symbols) onto plate stack, pop and match with food items (input tokens)
Factor like algebra: A→αβ1|αβ2 becomes A→αB, B→β1|β2 (pull common α outside)
A→Aα|β transforms to A→βA', A'→αA'|ε (convert right recursion after factoring)

analogies.analogy: Like assembling IKEA furniture: start with the instruction manual (start symbol), follow steps (productions) to build pieces (terminals) until complete.
Like guessing the next word in a sentence by context - you predict based on what you know without trying multiple possibilities.
Like a dictionary lookup: find the word (non-terminal) and its meaning (input token) to find the definition (production rule).
Like a snake eating its own tail - infinite loop because you keep expanding the same thing without progress.
FIRST is like the first letter of every word in a category; FOLLOW is like the letter that naturally comes after in alphabetizing.
